text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

================================================================================
PATIENT: Margaret Choi
MRN: [REDACTED]
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-XXXX-XXXXX
ATTENDING SURGEON: [REDACTED]
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [REDACTED], MD (Surgical Oncology)
CLINICAL SERVICE: Hepatopancreatobiliary Surgery
================================================================================

PROCEDURE: Pylorus-Preserving Pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple Procedure)

CLINICAL HISTORY:
The patient is a 58-year-old female (now 59 years old at the time of surgery) with a history of borderline resectable pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) of the pancreatic head/uncinate process, diagnosed via endoscopic ultrasound-guided fine needle biopsy. She received neoadjuvant modified FOLFIRINOX chemotherapy for 8 cycles followed by consolidative long-course concurrent chemoradiation (IMRT 50.4 Gy in 28 fractions with concurrent capecitabine). Restaging imaging after completion of chemoradiotherapy demonstrated maintained resectability with radiographic response; the tumor measured 2.2 cm (down from 3.8 cm at diagnosis). CA 19-9 at the time of surgery was 62 U/mL (down from 1,248 U/mL at diagnosis). The patient underwent pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy for curative intent resection.

================================================================================
SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
================================================================================
  A. Pancreaticoduodenectomy specimen (pylorus-preserving Whipple): Pancreatic head, uncinate process, duodenum (proximal), distal common bile duct, gallbladder, and regional peripancreatic soft tissue
  B. Superior mesenteric vein (SMV) wall segment (en bloc vascular resection, submitted separately)
  C. Additional SMA soft tissue margin (intraoperative frozen section, submitted for permanent section)
  D. Pancreatic neck margin (submitted separately by surgeon for frozen section; permanent section)

NOTE: Intraoperative frozen sections of the pancreatic neck margin and SMA margin were performed and reported as negative for malignancy intraoperatively; results confirmed on permanent section (see below).

================================================================================
GROSS DESCRIPTION:
================================================================================

SPECIMEN A - PANCREATICODUODENECTOMY (PYLORUS-PRESERVING WHIPPLE):
Received fresh, labeled with the patient's name and designated "Whipple specimen," is a pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy specimen. The specimen includes the pancreatic head and uncinate process, duodenum (approximately 24 cm in length), distal common bile duct (approximately 3.5 cm in length), cystic duct remnant (0.4 cm), and gallbladder (7.0 x 3.0 x 2.0 cm). The intact specimen measures 18 x 11 x 6.5 cm in aggregate and weighs 298 grams after fixation.

Gallbladder: The gallbladder wall is tan-pink and unremarkable. The serosa is smooth. The mucosa is green-yellow with normal velvety texture. No stones or polyps identified. Wall thickness 0.2 cm.

Common bile duct: The distal common bile duct is dilated (up to 1.0 cm in diameter), and a covered self-expanding metal biliary stent is present within the lumen. The stent is removed prior to sectioning. The bile duct mucosa is tan-yellow with no discrete lesions or strictures identified on gross inspection.

Duodenum: The duodenum (24 cm) is opened along its anti-mesenteric border. The mucosa is tan-pink with normal villous texture. No discrete masses, ulcers, or polyps are identified within the duodenum.

Pancreatic head and uncinate process: The specimen is serially sectioned perpendicular to the long axis of the pancreatic duct (axial sections, 4-5 mm intervals). The pancreatic parenchyma shows extensive architectural disruption with diffuse fibrosis and desmoplasia throughout the head and uncinate process. Within the pancreatic head, in the region of the main pancreatic duct, there is a firm, white-gray, stellate, poorly demarcated mass measuring 2.0 x 1.8 x 1.6 cm. The mass has an indurated, sclerotic cut surface with central chalky foci consistent with calcification (treatment effect). Surrounding parenchyma shows marked atrophy and fibrosis consistent with obstructive chronic pancreatitis and treatment-related changes. The main pancreatic duct, within the residual mass, is obliterated; proximally, the duct appears dilated with a diameter of approximately 4 mm. A 5-French plastic pancreatic duct stent (intraoperative) is noted at the margin of the pancreatic neck and is removed during dissection.

Ink designation (per College of American Pathologists protocol):
  - Anterior surface: Yellow ink
  - Posterior/retroperitoneal (uncinate/SMA) margin: Black ink
  - Pancreatic neck margin: Blue ink (transected surface)
  - Common bile duct margin: Green ink (transected surface)
  - Duodenal (proximal) margin: Not separately inked; noted at gross examination

The posterior (retroperitoneal/SMA) margin is grossly assessed: the mass appears to be at least 0.2-0.3 cm from the inked posterior margin in the sections examined; final microscopic measurement is reported below.

No peritoneal implants are identified on the serosal surfaces.

Lymph nodes: Multiple periduodenal, peripancreatic, superior mesenteric, and hepatoduodenal ligament lymph nodes are identified on gross dissection. In total, 24 lymph nodes are submitted for histologic examination. On gross examination, the majority are tan-white, 0.2-1.0 cm in greatest dimension; none demonstrate gross replacement by tumor.

SPECIMEN B - SMV WALL SEGMENT:
Received separately in formalin, labeled "SMV wall segment," is a flattened segment of vascular wall measuring 1.2 x 0.7 x 0.2 cm. The outer surface is adventitial fibrous tissue; the inner surface is smooth (endothelial surface). No tumor is grossly apparent.

SPECIMEN C - ADDITIONAL SMA SOFT TISSUE MARGIN:
Received in formalin, labeled "additional SMA margin," is a fragment of tan-pink fibrous and adipose soft tissue measuring 1.5 x 0.8 x 0.4 cm. No gross tumor is identified.

SPECIMEN D - PANCREATIC NECK MARGIN (FROZEN SECTION, PERMANENT):
Received in formalin following frozen section processing, labeled "pancreatic neck margin," is a disc of pancreatic parenchyma measuring 1.8 cm in diameter and 0.5 cm in thickness. Cut surface shows homogeneous pink-tan soft tissue with mild focal fibrosis. No gross tumor.

Representative sections submitted in cassettes A1 through A42, B1, C1, D1-D2.

================================================================================
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION AND DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS:
================================================================================

RESIDUAL TUMOR CHARACTERISTICS:
Sections through the pancreatic head mass demonstrate residual moderately differentiated ductal adenocarcinoma set within a background of extensive treatment effect. The residual carcinoma is characterized by irregular, angulated, and haphazardly arranged glandular structures embedded within an abundant desmoplastic stroma. The neoplastic glands exhibit moderate nuclear pleomorphism, prominent nucleoli, amphophilic to mucinous cytoplasm, and loss of normal ductal polarity. Focal intraluminal necrotic debris ("dirty necrosis") is present in some neoplastic glands.

TREATMENT EFFECT:
Extensive treatment-related changes are present throughout the primary tumor bed, including:
  - Dense hyalinized fibrosis replacing the majority of the original tumor bed
  - Dystrophic calcifications (punctate to confluent)
  - Foamy macrophage infiltrates (hemosiderin-laden histiocytes)
  - Acinar atrophy and replacement by fibrous tissue
  - Scattered ghost cells and cellular debris within areas of hyalinization
  - Rare foci of fat necrosis

Tumor regression grading (College of American Pathologists [CAP] grading system):
  Grade 2 (Moderate Response): Residual carcinoma with prominent fibrosis (treatment effect). Viable tumor is present in approximately 30% of the lesion bed. This corresponds to a moderate treatment response and is not a pathologic complete response.

The viable carcinoma is distributed in multiple discrete foci within the primary lesion bed. Tumor foci are identified in the pancreatic head and uncinate process. No satellite nodules are identified outside the primary lesion bed.

MARGIN STATUS:
  - Pancreatic neck margin (Specimen D / permanent from frozen): NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY. The pancreatic neck margin shows normal to mildly atrophic pancreatic parenchyma with no evidence of invasive carcinoma. Clearance: 0.4 cm from the nearest viable tumor focus.
  - Common bile duct margin: NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY. The resected bile duct margin demonstrates benign ductal epithelium with mild reactive changes (biliary stent effect: focal epithelial denudation, submucosal edema, and mild chronic inflammation). No carcinoma in situ or invasive adenocarcinoma at the margin.
  - Posterior/retroperitoneal (uncinate/SMA) margin: NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY. The inked posterior margin is clear of invasive carcinoma. Nearest viable tumor focus to this margin measures 0.2 cm.
  - Anterior surface: NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY. No tumor at the anterior serosal surface.
  - Proximal duodenal margin: NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY.

RESECTION CLASSIFICATION: R0 (microscopically negative margins at all resection planes)

LYMPH NODES:
  Total lymph nodes examined: 24
  Positive lymph nodes: 4 of 24

  Positive nodes are identified from the peripancreatic and periduodenal nodal groups. Metastatic foci in involved lymph nodes show moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma with desmoplastic reaction, consistent with the primary tumor morphology. The largest metastatic deposit measures 0.9 cm. No extranodal extension is identified in the positive lymph nodes examined. The remaining 20 lymph nodes show reactive changes (follicular hyperplasia, sinus histiocytosis) without evidence of malignancy.

PERINEURAL INVASION: PRESENT. Multiple foci of perineural invasion are identified, involving small and medium-caliber nerve trunks within the peripancreatic soft tissue of the uncinate process and retroperitoneal adipose tissue.

LYMPHOVASCULAR INVASION: NOT IDENTIFIED on routine H&E-stained sections. (See ancillary studies below.)

GALLBLADDER:
No histologic abnormality. The gallbladder wall shows mild chronic cholecystitis with mural fibrosis. Mucosa is unremarkable. No carcinoma, dysplasia, or stones. Cystic duct margin: negative.

SMV WALL SEGMENT (Specimen B):
Sections demonstrate fibrous adventitia with focal organizing thrombus on the intimal surface. No viable carcinoma is identified within the vascular wall specimen. Findings consistent with reactive fibrovascular changes.

ADDITIONAL SMA SOFT TISSUE MARGIN (Specimen C):
Fibro-adipose tissue with no evidence of invasive carcinoma. Scattered small nerves with reactive perineural changes. Margin: negative.

================================================================================
ANCILLARY STUDIES:
================================================================================

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on residual viable tumor in Specimen A):
  - CK7: Positive (diffuse, strong)
  - CK20: Negative
  - CDX2: Negative
  - MUC1: Positive (luminal and cytoplasmic)
  - MUC2: Negative
  - MUC5AC: Positive (focal)
  - SMAD4 (DPC4): NEGATIVE (loss of expression) -- confirms loss of SMAD4 protein, concordant with index biopsy and molecular findings
  - p53: Aberrant overexpression (diffuse, strong nuclear positivity) -- consistent with TP53 gain-of-function mutation
  - Ki-67 (MIB-1): Approximately 20% of residual viable tumor nuclei positive (decreased from 35% on index diagnostic biopsy, reflecting treatment effect on proliferating tumor cells)
  - MLH1: Retained nuclear expression
  - MSH2: Retained nuclear expression
  - MSH6: Retained nuclear expression
  - PMS2: Retained nuclear expression
  (Mismatch repair: Proficient, pMMR -- concordant with prior molecular profiling showing MSI-stable status)

PENDING STUDIES:
  - D2-40 (podoplanin) immunohistochemical stain for lymphovascular invasion assessment: PENDING (result to be added as addendum)
  - ERG immunohistochemical stain for vascular endothelial differentiation: PENDING (result to be added as addendum)
  - Additional FISH or reflex molecular studies: Not currently ordered; clinical team may request if indicated for treatment planning

================================================================================
GROSS PATHOLOGY PHOTOGRAPHS:
================================================================================
Gross photographs of the intact specimen, bisected cross-sections showing the primary tumor bed, and margin surfaces were obtained and are archived in the digital pathology image database.

================================================================================
PATHOLOGIC STAGING (Post-Neoadjuvant, AJCC 8th Edition):
================================================================================
Primary tumor (ypT): ypT2 -- Tumor limited to the pancreas, >2 cm in greatest dimension (residual viable tumor within treatment-modified lesion bed measuring 2.0 x 1.8 x 1.6 cm; treated as largest dimension of viable carcinoma)

Note: The overall lesion bed measures 2.0 cm in greatest dimension; the "y" prefix designates post-neoadjuvant therapy pathologic staging. Per AJCC 8th edition, ypT stage is based on the residual viable tumor extent.

Regional lymph nodes (ypN): ypN2 -- 4 or more positive regional lymph nodes (4 of 24 lymph nodes positive for metastatic adenocarcinoma)

Distant metastasis (M): MX (not assessed pathologically in this specimen; no evidence of distant disease on preoperative imaging)

Pathologic Stage: ypT2 ypN2 cM0 -- Stage III (post-neoadjuvant)

CAP Tumor Regression Grade: Grade 2 (Moderate response; residual carcinoma with extensive fibrosis)

Resection Status: R0 (all margins negative microscopically)

================================================================================
DIAGNOSIS:
================================================================================

A. PYLORUS-PRESERVING PANCREATICODUODENECTOMY (WHIPPLE) SPECIMEN:
   1. RESIDUAL MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED DUCTAL ADENOCARCINOMA, PANCREATIC HEAD/UNCINATE PROCESS, with extensive treatment effect (CAP Tumor Regression Grade 2: Moderate response; approximately 30% viable tumor)
   2. Tumor size (residual lesion bed): 2.0 x 1.8 x 1.6 cm
   3. Perineural invasion: PRESENT
   4. Lymphovascular invasion: NOT IDENTIFIED (D2-40 immunostain pending, see addendum)
   5. Margins: ALL NEGATIVE (R0 resection)
      - Pancreatic neck margin: Negative (0.4 cm clearance)
      - Common bile duct margin: Negative
      - Posterior/retroperitoneal (SMA/uncinate) margin: Negative (0.2 cm clearance)
      - Anterior surface: Negative
      - Duodenal margin: Negative
   6. Lymph nodes: 4 of 24 POSITIVE for metastatic adenocarcinoma (ypN2)
   7. Gallbladder: Mild chronic cholecystitis; no malignancy

B. SMV WALL SEGMENT: Fibrous adventitia with organizing thrombus; NO CARCINOMA IDENTIFIED

C. ADDITIONAL SMA SOFT TISSUE MARGIN: Fibro-adipose tissue; NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY

D. PANCREATIC NECK MARGIN (FROZEN, PERMANENT): NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY

PATHOLOGIC STAGE (AJCC 8th edition, post-neoadjuvant):
ypT2  ypN2  cM0  --  Stage III

================================================================================
COMMENT:
================================================================================
The histologic findings are consistent with residual moderately differentiated pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma following neoadjuvant modified FOLFIRINOX chemotherapy and consolidative chemoradiation (capecitabine-based). Treatment effect is prominent and widespread, comprising dense hyalinized fibrosis, calcification, and macrophage infiltration. The residual viable carcinoma comprises approximately 30% of the lesion bed, corresponding to a CAP tumor regression Grade 2 (moderate response).

Smad4 (DPC4) loss is confirmed on the surgical resection specimen by IHC, concordant with the initial diagnostic biopsy. Loss of SMAD4 expression is associated with a higher risk of systemic recurrence and may have prognostic implications in the post-resection setting.

The finding of 4 positive regional lymph nodes (ypN2) despite neoadjuvant treatment represents a high-risk pathologic feature. R0 resection was achieved.

Mismatch repair proteins (MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2) are retained, confirming proficient mismatch repair (pMMR) status, consistent with prior molecular profiling.

This case has been reviewed by two pathologists. Results are concordant.

D2-40 and ERG immunostains for lymphovascular invasion assessment are pending; results will be reported in an addendum.

================================================================================
SIGNING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED], MD
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine

CO-SIGNING / REVIEWING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED], MD
Gastrointestinal/Hepatopancreatobiliary Pathology
================================================================================

Report status: FINAL (pending D2-40/ERG addendum)

================================================================================
ADDENDUM (issued upon completion of pending studies):

D2-40 (podoplanin) immunostain: Highlights lymphatic channels within the specimen; NO UNEQUIVOCAL LYMPHOVASCULAR INVASION is identified in residual viable tumor foci on D2-40-stained sections. ERG immunostain: Highlights vascular endothelium; no carcinoma within vascular spaces identified. FINAL DIAGNOSIS UNCHANGED: Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified.
================================================================================
